content: Requested a strict proof of a necessary-and-sufficient condition for Minkowski convexity of F_{m,L}(K)=V(K[n-m],L[m])/V(K[n-1],L), with a screenshot containing only the first half of the theorem statement.

content: Formalized the problem as a classification question in (n, m) and drafted project goals G1-G2. Proof work remains gated on explicit user approval of the goals.
provenance: user instruction in Codex thread on 2026-05-30 Asia/Shanghai

content: Approved the proposed goals for the classification and working-paper writeup.
provenance: user message '同意' in Codex thread on 2026-05-30 Asia/Shanghai

content: Activated proof workstream WS-G1-001-classify-minkowski-convexity-of-f-m-l. Current candidate classification is 1 <= m <= n, with an explicit box counterexample for m=0 and a positive-case proof route through mixed-volume quotient convexity.
uncertainty: The positive-case proof depends on a Hodge-Riemann/Alexandrov-Fenchel quotient-convexity input that still needs independent reviewer verification against sources.

content: Reviewer loop blocked the broad positive-case proof. Workstream WS-G1-001 now records m=0 as disproved, m=1 and m=n as verified positive cases, and 2<=m<=n-1 as the active proof gap. Fixed-seed computation logs were added for boxes and zonotopes.
uncertainty: Candidate classification 1<=m<=n remains unproved for intermediate cases.

content: Added a rigorous Lorentzian bilinear-form proof of the n=3,m=2 intermediate case. The classification is now fully verified for n=2 and n=3; the active gap is n>=4 with 2<=m<=n-1.
uncertainty: The higher-order analogue needed for all remaining intermediate cases is still not proved or sourced.

content: Checked quotient-concavity literature routes. Collins gives an exact formal Hessian quotient analogue but only on a solvable set; nearby mixed-volume inequalities from Lehmann-Xiao and Kerner-Nemethi do not yet close the full-cone convex-body gap.
uncertainty: The remaining proof may require a new full-cone convex-body Hessian quotient argument or a counterexample in dimension at least 4.

content: Added a fixed-seed local Hessian search in shared-direction zonotope cones. The search found no local convexity violation in tested n=4,5 intermediate cases, but it remains only computational evidence.
uncertainty: Finite random searches cannot replace the missing higher-order Hodge-Riemann proof.

content: Revised the classification after finding bivariate volume-polynomial counterexamples: universal Minkowski convexity now appears to hold exactly for m=1, m=n, and the exceptional case (n,m)=(3,2). All n>=4 intermediate cases are disproved in the updated report.
uncertainty: The revised report still needs an independent reviewer gate before it can be marked complete.

content: Citation review blocked the revised report only on missing positive-case source specificity. The report and n=3,m=2 artifact now cite exact local passages from Wang and Branden-Leake for those inputs.
uncertainty: Awaiting citation re-review before completion gate can pass.

content: Citation follow-up approved the revised report's source alignment. Obsolete blocking review JSON files were archived into a durable markdown note so the active completion gate reflects the current approved reviews.
Workstream WS-G1-001 passed completion gate and was marked complete. Project moved to final rendering phase.
Rendered final working paper from the reviewed workstream report and marked the project complete.
uncertainty: The working paper is not a formalized Lean proof and does not include explicit vertex coordinates for Shephard's realizing polytopes.
